root_text.an4.39:1.1: Atha kho ujjayo brāhmaṇo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavatā saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an4.39:1.2: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho ujjayo brāhmaṇo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an4.39:1.4: “Na kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ yaññaṁ vaṇṇemi;
root_text.an4.39:1.5: na panāhaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sabbaṁ yaññaṁ na vaṇṇemi.
root_text.an4.39:1.6: Yathārūpe kho, brāhmaṇa, yaññe gāvo haññanti, ajeḷakā haññanti, kukkuṭasūkarā haññanti, vividhā pāṇā saṅghātaṁ āpajjanti;
root_text.an4.39:1.7: evarūpaṁ kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, sārambhaṁ yaññaṁ na vaṇṇemi.
root_text.an4.39:1.9: Evarūpañhi, brāhmaṇa, sārambhaṁ yaññaṁ na upasaṅkamanti arahanto vā arahattamaggaṁ vā samāpannā.
root_text.an4.39:2.1: Yathārūpe ca kho, brāhmaṇa, yaññe neva gāvo haññanti, na ajeḷakā haññanti, na kukkuṭasūkarā haññanti, na vividhā pāṇā saṅghātaṁ āpajjanti;
root_text.an4.39:2.2: evarūpaṁ kho ahaṁ, brāhmaṇa, nirārambhaṁ yaññaṁ vaṇṇemi, yadidaṁ niccadānaṁ anukulayaññaṁ.
root_text.an4.39:2.4: Evarūpañhi, brāhmaṇa, nirārambhaṁ yaññaṁ upasaṅkamanti arahanto vā arahattamaggaṁ vā samāpannāti.

translation_text.an4.39:1.1: Then Ujjaya the brahmin went up to the Buddha, and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.an4.39:1.2: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to the Buddha:
translation_text.an4.39:1.5: Nor do I criticize all sacrifices.
translation_text.an4.39:1.6: Take the kind of sacrifice where cattle, goats and sheep, chickens and pigs, and various kinds of creatures are slaughtered.
translation_text.an4.39:1.7: I criticize that kind of violent sacrifice.
translation_text.an4.39:1.9: Because neither perfected ones nor those who are on the path to perfection will attend such a violent sacrifice.
translation_text.an4.39:2.1: But take the kind of sacrifice where cattle, goats and sheep, chickens and pigs, and various kinds of creatures are not slaughtered.
translation_text.an4.39:2.2: I praise that kind of non-violent sacrifice; for example, a regular gift as an propitious sacrifice.
translation_text.an4.39:2.4: Because perfected ones and those who are on the path to perfection will attend such a non-violent sacrifice.
translation_text.an4.39:3.2: the sacrifices of the ‘casting of the yoke-pin’,
translation_text.an4.39:3.4: these huge violent sacrifices yield no great fruit.
translation_text.an4.39:4.1: The great sages of right comportment
translation_text.an4.39:5.1: But the great sages of right comportment
translation_text.an4.39:5.5: and various creatures are not killed.
translation_text.an4.39:6.1: An intelligent person should sacrifice like this,
translation_text.an4.39:6.2: for this sacrifice is very fruitful.
translation_text.an4.39:6.3: For a sponsor of sacrifices like this,
translation_text.an4.39:6.5: Such a sacrifice is truly abundant,
translation_text.an4.39:6.6: and even the deities are pleased.”

variant_text.an4.39:3.3: Mahāyaññā mahārambhā → sammāpāsaṁ vācapeyyaṁ; niraggalaṁ mahārambhā (pts1ed)
variant_text.an4.39:5.4: vividhā nettha haññare → vividhā yattha haññare (sya-all, km)
